title: Little Red Riding Hood
author: Traditional
description: A little girl goes to visit her grandmother, but a wolf has a bad plan.

vocabularies.meaning: a large area with many trees
a wild animal like a large dog
a piece of cloth worn over the shoulders
the hard white things in your mouth
to look for animals to catch or kill

scenes.title: Introduction
scenes.narrator: Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She always wore a red cape. So everyone called her Little Red Riding Hood.

dialogue.speaker: Mother
dialogue.line: Your grandmother is sick. Please take this basket to her.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Yes, mother! I will go right now.

dialogue.speaker: Mother
dialogue.line: Be careful. Do not talk to strangers in the forest.

scenes.title: Meeting the Wolf
scenes.narrator: Red walked through the forest. She met a wolf.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Good morning, little girl. Where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: I am going to my grandmother's house. She is sick.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Where does your grandmother live?

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: She lives in the big house at the end of the road.

scenes.title: Wolf's Plan
scenes.narrator: The wolf had a bad idea. He wanted to eat Red and her grandmother.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: I will go to the grandmother's house first!

scenes.title: The Race
scenes.narrator: Red stopped to pick some flowers. The wolf ran to grandmother's house.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Look at these beautiful flowers! Grandmother will like them.

scenes.title: Wolf at the Door
scenes.narrator: The wolf arrived at grandmother's house. He knocked on the door.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Knock, knock! Grandmother, it is me, Red!

dialogue.speaker: Grandmother
dialogue.line: Who is it?

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: It is Red! I brought you some food.

dialogue.speaker: Grandmother
dialogue.line: Come in, dear. The door is open.

scenes.title: Wolf Eats Grandmother
scenes.narrator: The wolf went inside. He ate the grandmother! Then he put on her clothes and got into her bed.

scenes.title: Red Arrives
scenes.narrator: Red arrived at the house. She went inside.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Grandmother, I am here!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Come in, dear. I am in bed.

scenes.title: Something is Wrong
scenes.narrator: Red looked at her grandmother. Something was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Grandmother, what big ears you have!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: The better to hear you with, dear.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Grandmother, what big eyes you have!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: The better to see you with, dear.

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Grandmother, what big teeth you have!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: The better to eat you with!

scenes.title: The Wolf Catches Red
scenes.narrator: The wolf jumped up and ate Red too!

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Ahhh! Help me!

scenes.title: The Woodcutter
scenes.narrator: A woodcutter walked by the house. He heard a strange noise.

dialogue.speaker: Woodcutter
dialogue.line: What is that noise? I must go inside.

scenes.title: The Rescue
scenes.narrator: The woodcutter saw the wolf sleeping. He cut open the wolf's stomach and found Red and her grandmother. They were alive!

dialogue.speaker: Red
dialogue.line: Thank you! You saved us!

dialogue.speaker: Woodcutter
dialogue.line: Be careful in the forest. Do not talk to strangers.

scenes.title: Happy Ending
scenes.narrator: Red and her grandmother were safe. They learned to be careful. Red never talked to strangers again.
